master_row.title: Quantum Tunneling Probability Tunnel Relation
master_row.canonical_equation: $, the quantum tunneling probability is \begin{equation} P_{tunnel} = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} A_n \exp\left(-\frac{S_n}{\hbar}\right) \end{equation} where the instanton action \begin{equation} S_n = n \int_{d_{min}}^{d_{bolt}} \sqrt{2m_{eff}U(d')} \, dd' \end{equation} \end{theorem} \begin{proof} Apply WKB approximation to time-dependent Schrödinger equation in imaginary time $
master_row.formation_inputs: ħ, n, A_n, S_n, w, h
master_row.dictionary: Quantum Tunneling Probability Tunnel Relation —  the quantum tunneling probability is P_tunnel = ∑_n=1^∞ A_n exp (-(S_n)/(ħ) ) where the instanton action S_n = n ∫_d_min^d_bolt sqrt2m_effU(d') dd' A
master_row.encyclopedia: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `00_Engine\Lemma-Maker\Prism\topological_quantum_storage_paper.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.
